thig13.5:1.1: “I was a child, with clean clothes,
thig13.5:1.2: when I first heard the Dhamma.
thig13.5:1.4: was a break-through to the truth.
thig13.5:2.1: Then I arrived at an enormous dissatisfaction
thig13.5:5.1: it wouldn’t be proper for me—
thig13.5:6.2: don’t buy awakening, don’t buy peace.
thig13.5:8.1: It’s to this extent that many, many men —heedless, their hearts defiled—
thig13.5:10.1:
So, my relatives:
Why do you, like enemies,
thig13.5:10.2: try to bind me to sensuality?
thig13.5:11.2: can’t put an end to effluents.
thig13.5:11.3: Sensuality is an enemy, a murderer,
thig13.5:12.1:
So, my relatives:
Why do you, like enemies,
thig13.5:12.2: try to bind me to sensuality?
thig13.5:12.4: with shaven head, wrapped in a patchwork cloak.
thig13.5:13.2: a robe made from cast-off cloth:
thig13.5:13.4: the requisites of one with no home.
thig13.5:14.1: The great seers have rejected sensuality,
thig13.5:14.3: Released are they, in the place of security.
thig13.5:14.4: Arrived are they, in unshaken ease.
thig13.5:15.1: So may I not come into union
thig13.5:15.2: with sensuality, in which no shelter is found.
thig13.5:15.4:
painful, like a mass of flame.
Greed:
thig13.5:16.4:
a great cause of delusion.
Sensuality:
thig13.5:17.4:
blinded, run-of-the-mill.
Because many people in the world
thig13.5:18.1: are stuck in the mud of sensuality,
thig13.5:18.3: they don’t realize the ending of birth & death.
thig13.5:19.1: Many people follow the path to bad destinations
thig13.5:20.3: It’s the bait of the world,
thig13.5:21.3: It’s a net cast by Māra
thig13.5:21.4: for the defilement of living beings:
thig13.5:22.4: drying up the good side [of the mind].
thig13.5:23.1: I, having cast off much trouble like this
thig13.5:23.4: as I always delight in unbinding.
thig13.5:24.2: in hopes of the cool state,
thig13.5:24.3: I will stay heedful, finding delight
thig13.5:24.4:
in the ending of fetters.
I follow the path— eightfold, straight,
thig13.5:25.4:
over which great seers have crossed.”
See this Subhā, the goldsmith’s daughter,
thig13.5:26.4: doing jhāna at the foot of a tree.
thig13.5:27.1: This is the eighth day of her going forth
thig13.5:27.2: confident, beautiful through the true Dhamma.
thig13.5:27.4: she’s a three-knowledge woman who’s left death behind;
thig13.5:29.1: Sakka, lord of beings, with his community of devas,
